title: ACT Manifest — locales block (PRD-104)
description: Shape of the optional `locales` block on the ACT manifest envelope. Owned by PRD-104. Sibling PRD (manifest envelope, gap-A1 / wire-format) owns the surrounding manifest object; this schema covers only the `locales` sub-object.

default.description: Default BCP-47 locale tag (e.g. "en-US"). REQUIRED when the `locales` block is present. Pattern is a deliberate v0.1 subset of BCP-47 — full BCP-47 admits extlangs, multiple variants, private-use tags, and grandfathered tags that v0.1 does not need to model. Producers needing a full-BCP-47 tag SHOULD validate it out-of-band; consumers MUST accept whatever string passes the subset regex.

available.description: Closed-content list of BCP-47 locale tags this producer ships. MUST include `default`. Adding a value to this array is producer-side data, not a spec change; the array shape itself is MINOR-extensible at the spec level per PRD-108 R4(1).

manifest_url_template.description: OPTIONAL URL template containing the `{locale}` placeholder. When present, consumers fetch the per-locale manifest by substituting a value from `available`. When omitted, the same `index_url` / `node_url_template` from the parent manifest are reused with locale-prefixed IDs (Pattern 1).

$comment: Cross-validation rule (default MUST appear in available) is expressed in the surrounding manifest schema or by PRD-600's validator, not by this fragment alone — JSON Schema 2020-12 cannot express that constraint without a `$dynamicRef` to the runtime value of `default`. PRD-104-R3 makes the rule normative.
